entry.title: Operational Reporting on SAP S/4HANA live data
entry.content:
Summary

This article describes architecture options for SAP S/4HANA operational reporting with live data needs. Analytical applications should access data directly from the application tables in SAP S/4HANA, avoiding data replication to another application due to latency concerns. 

Possible options for this scenario are the following:

  • SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs
  • SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA analytical CDS Views (Queries) for flexible self-service reporting via SAP Query Browser

Business Requirements

Business users need an accurate, real-time picture of the current business situation based on KPIs. Real-time analytics and reporting capabilities in SAP S/4HANA are needed to fulfil the business requirements.

Requirements may be linked to business process steps or should to be provided as separate applications (e.g., outstanding payments of a specific customer for the last 12 months) with flexible slice-and-dice capabilities.

Operational reports/analytical applications should be accessible based on S/4HANA user roles integrated into SAP Fiori Launchpad.

Solution Options

Most commonly used options for the operational reporting scenario on live data are the following:

  • SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs
  • SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA CDS Views (Queries) for flexible self-service reporting via SAP Query Browser

These solution options are applicable for SAP S/4HANA public Cloud, SAP S/4HANA private Cloud or SAP S/4HANA on premise deployments.

Characterization of the Solution Options
 
 SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs

SAP S/4HANA (on-premise, private or public Cloud) provides out of the box reporting functionality via SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics. It is a fully integrated analytics solution based on SAP Fiori and is included in standard SAP S/4HANA license or subscription fee.

How to use SAP Embedded Analytics?
SAP S/4HANA embedded analytic applications are based on SAP Fiori (Elements) technology and can be accessed via SAP Fiori Launchpad.
SAP provides several types of analytic apps, such as:

  • Overview pages
  • Analytic List Pages
  • KPI Tiles
  • Multidimensional Reporting Client+
  • Review Booklets
  • Multidimensional Analysis

More than 500 predefined Analytical SAP Fiori Apps are provided and can be accessed in SAP Fiori Launchpad as long as they have been generally activated and you are assigned to the role, which includes the app.

Additional information about SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics can be found here:

  • SAP Help
  • SAP Learning: Going Deeper with SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics 
    (free of charge 10h virtual learning class)

ABAP Core data services (CDS) is the backbone of this solution and provide access to the data in the raw data tables in SAP S/4HANA. No additional persistency is required and CDS views are purely virtual structures.
SAP provides a wide range of predefined analytical Fiori Apps supporting over 20 industries and 50 different lines of business. A full list can be found via SAP Fiori Application Reference Library


After customer has chosen the analytical Fiori applications and activated them in the S/4HANA System, 
business users are able to personalize these Fiori applications by using variants or creating own tiles.
Power user are even more flexible and able to adjust and extend most of the SAP Fiori applications in a graphical editor.  
Details see: Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation.

It is also possible to create own SAP Fiori application for analytic purpose based on predefined floor plans, such as overview page or analytical list page.
Developing Apps with SAP Fiori Elements -> Developing Apps with SAP Fiori Elements

 SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is recommended for advanced reporting applications, dashboards or planning applications beyond the capabilities provided by SAP Fiori.
As the strategic Reporting & Planning solution from SAP, SAC makes it easy to visualize data based on your own needs and corporate design guidelines.

Besides the “data import connection” SAC provides the so-called “live connection” for other SAP solutions. 'Live connections' are widely used by SAP customers and do not replicate application data into the cloud and keep the data transfer within your coprporate network.
SAC mainly serves as design time for the reporting application. At runtime only meta data is provided by SAC. The data itself for the report is directly fetched from the SAP source system (e.g. SAP S/4HANA) and sent directly to the browser of the business user without leaving the corporate network.
This is a unique differentiator compared to other non-SAP reporting solutions. With this approach business users connect with their SAP S/4HANA backend user to the S/4HANA system and utilizing the existing authorization concept.
For details, please check out SAP Analytics Cloud Connectivity Guidelines.

How to use SAP Analytics Cloud with live connectivity from SAP S/4HANA side for operational reporting:

  • Execute SAP S/4HANA Fiori App ‘Manage KPIs and Reports’
  • Go to ‘Stories’
  • Create Story (SAC needs to be configured before in SAP S/4HANA according to description Manage KPIs and Reports - Stories )

SAC integrates with SAP S/4HANA via analytical CDS views and InA protocol (similar like a BW query) or via OData. The SAC story is served with data directly from the CDS view at runtime.

Modern Dashboard design via SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

SAP also provides predefined SAC Dashboards based on SAP S/4HANA live data. Those scenarios are listed in the Analytics Content Network:

Source: Extended Content Inventory

Customers can also define their own SAC stories based on SAP S/4HANA (analytical) CDS views. It is even possible to blend data between two SAP S/4HANA systems (SAP Analytics Cloud Help - Blend Data ).

SAC stories can be integrated seamlessly from UI perspective into the SAP Fiori Launchpad framework. Shared UI paradigms such as intent-based navigation optimize the experience for SAP S/4HANA business users. It is e.g. possible to jump from a sales order number within the SAC story directly into the SAP Fiori app for maintaining this specific sales order (SAP Fiori app: Manage Sales Order).
Details see: SAP S/4HANA on premise: Integration of SAP Analytics Cloud optimizes business user experience.

Remark: This intend-based navigation and the support of variants are very strong features and key differentiators for SAC. 

 Analytical CDS Views (queries) for flexible self-service reporting via
 SAP Query Browser

The third option for operational reporting which is introduced in this decision paper is related to flexible querying on S/4HANA live data.
Customers also call this option Self-Service Querying, similar like in the past with SAP Query with Infosets in SAP ECC.

How to use SAP Query Browser:

  • Execute the Fiori app ‘Query Browser’
  • Choose the specific analytic CDS View / query which you are interested in
  • Execute and 'Open for Analysis'
  • After entering the multidimensional Reporting Client querying can be done in pivot-table like style.

This option provided a simple way for table-based adhoc reporting with slice and dice functionality and limited graphical reporting features.

 

For further information about embedded analytics in general, including usage of reporting apps, Query Browser and SAC please take a look to the SAP Learning: Going Deeper with SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics

Decision Tree

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics (Standard Fiori apps or generic Query Browser) and SAC provide an excellent base for operational reporting on SAP S/4HANA live data.

All these three options can be seamlessly integrated into the SAP Fiori Launchpad, utilize existing user setups/authorizations in SAP S/4HANA, and do not require additional persistency or data flow logic. Development, operations, and maintenance costs are low, with minimal development effort compared to other solutions.

Context information from SAP S/4HANA, such as hierarchy/currency/unit/time conversions, is reused without additional effort.

Review Booklets and a new Multidimensional Analytics (MDA) client are under development, further enriching SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics capabilities (see SAP S/4HANA product roadmap).

entry.title: Re: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content:
Datasphere allows us to merge the outputs of 12 nodes using a single union operation. This streamlined approach eliminates the need for multiple unions, simplifying the view while ensuring that all node outputs are consolidated into one cohesive view.

Thanks,

AS

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud

entry.title: How to read and parse SAMLResponse in custom Odata read call
entry.content:
Hello All,

Can you please suggest if SAML Response can be read in a custom Odata Read call in below scenario.

1. When user logs in to the portal, SSO authentication is done. During this authentication process, the logged in user is changed based on the configuration in USREXTID table. For example, if user A is maintained as substitute of user B, then user A will access all applications as user B. This is as per SAML configurations.

2. After authentication, while loading the landing page in Fiori there is a Odata call to backend to fetch user attributes. In this call, we are able to fetch attributes of only UserB (as per above configuration). But in this call, we also want to read the actual user ID who is accessing the application. Is it possible to read SAML response or NameID attribute in odata read call.

3. We do not want to read the data from table USREXTID, because same user can have multiple substitute users. Hence, we want to know if its possible to read and parse SAMLresponse.

Best Regards!
